name: Release Framework Capability -- PREPARE ONLY (Release, handover F-015, HUMAN GATE H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE)

design_notes.what_this_feature_is: Feature 045 is the phase's FINAL, human-gated feature. It PREPARES the v0.7.0 framework release package (CHANGELOG.md consolidation, a release-metadata bump across NIZAM.json/docs/guide/index.html/README.md/CONTEXT.md, a minimum-scope guide update naming the shipped ecosystem/ module, and a release-readiness evidence checklist) but does NOT self-cut the tag, does NOT self-approve the human sign-off, and does NOT publish a GitHub Release. Per the house release-mechanics precedent (methodology/06_release_train.md Sec 6; the real chore(release) commits 955c1d7/09a7a7d/5a20fe0), tag creation and sign-off are release-manager/human-operator actions performed AFTER this contract's implementation lands and the orchestrator relays the operator's verbatim H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE decision. This contract's own scope ends at a reviewable, human-readable release package; 'Human sign-off complete' and 'Immutable tag published' (feature_list_005.json's own acceptance tests 3 and 4) are explicitly GATE-DEPENDENT and are NEVER satisfied, simulated, or marked PASS by this contract or its implementation step.
design_notes.version_classification_analysis: Classified MINOR (0.6.0 -> 0.7.0), matching product_spec_005 Sec 8's own expected classification, independently re-derived here from the REAL diff surface (git diff --stat origin/main..HEAD, origin/main pinned at 5a20fe0, the last commit BEFORE this branch's 20 phase-005 commits; verified live at contract-authoring time): every shipped-surface change is a NEW file or a strictly ADDITIVE edit to an existing one -- ecosystem/README.md + 4 new ecosystem/*.md protocol docs (new directory, new module); 3 new schema/*.schema.json files + `schema/README.md`'s existing Schemas table gains 3 new rows (no existing row edited or removed); `tools/ecosystem_preflight.py` (new file); `tools/validate.sh` gains check **C12** (default sweep migrates 11->12 passed; live-verified `bash tools/validate.sh` now reports `SUMMARY: 12 passed, 0 failed`); `tools/skill.json` gains 4 new capability entries (version already self-bumped 0.1.1->0.2.0 by feature 040, additive-capability MINOR precedent); `NIZAM.json` gains 1 new module entry (`ecosystem`), 4 new capability entries, and 3 new schema index entries -- zero existing NIZAM.json entries removed or renamed; `docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md` (new). CRITICALLY, no CONSUMER-FACING breaking change exists: `bootstrap.sh` itself is byte-unmodified this whole phase (git diff --stat origin/main..HEAD touches zero lines of it); the injected 4-directory consumer payload (`standard/`, `templates/`, `schema/`, `tools/`) only gained NEW files, no existing payload file's behavior regressed; and -- the decisive, live-verified compatibility proof -- `bash tools/validate.sh --payload` still reports `SUMMARY (payload mode): 10 passed, 0 failed`, IDENTICAL to the pre-phase-005 count, because C12 is deliberately full-sweep-only (never invoked in `--payload` mode, confirmed by direct inspection of validate.sh's payload-mode branch, which runs exactly C1-C5,C7-C11). `ecosystem/` itself is a framework-side-only module (like `methodology/`, `registry/`, `docs/`), never part of `bootstrap.sh`'s injected set, per CONTEXT.md's own Execution Commands section and product_spec_005 Sec 1/2.2's explicit 'consumer adoption deferred' framing. No MAJOR-triggering removal, rename, or behavior change exists anywhere in the diff; PATCH is ruled out because genuinely new capability surface (a module, 3 schemas, a CLI, a validator check) shipped, not merely a fix. Conclusion: MINOR is the only semver-correct classification, subject to the human operator's own confirmation per H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE (this contract's design_notes constitute the analysis the orchestrator relays for that confirmation, not a self-executed decision).
design_notes.release_notes_deliverable_correction: The orchestrator's dispatch requested a 'release-notes document for v0.7.0 (tracked, at the house location)'. Investigation of the REAL house process corrects this: `.github/workflows/release.yml` (already merged to `origin/main` at 5a20fe0, PR #19, BEFORE this branch diverged) mechanizes GitHub Release publication by extracting the pushed tag's OWN `## [X.Y.Z]` CHANGELOG.md section verbatim -- there is no separate tracked release-notes file in the current (post-#19) house process; CHANGELOG.md's per-version section IS the release-notes source of truth going forward. The untracked `v0.6.0-release-notes.md` at the repo root is a vestige of the OLDER, PRE-automation manual process (used before release.yml existed, when a human hand-drafted the GitHub Release body) -- it has already been presented to, and tolerated by, the human operator TWICE as a dogfood preflight exception (run_state.json events for features 043 and 044, both under the NDEBT-013/014 friction class) and is explicitly NOT this feature's file to create-a-successor-of, edit, or delete. This contract's CHANGELOG.md `## [0.7.0]` deliverable satisfies the release-notes requirement directly; no new `v0.7.0-release-notes.md` (or similarly named) file is created.
design_notes.changelog_fold_procedure: Mirrors the exact, live-verified precedent of the three real chore(release) commits (955c1d7 for v0.6.0, and the analogous folds for earlier tags): CHANGELOG.md's `## [Unreleased]` header is left PERMANENTLY EMPTY (Keep-a-Changelog convention, ready for the next dev cycle) and exactly one new line, `## [0.7.0] - 2026-07-17`, is inserted directly beneath it and directly above the existing `## [0.6.0] - 2026-07-13` heading. The content CURRENTLY sitting under `[Unreleased]` (the release.yml/ROADMAP-0.2.0/DEBT-0.8.0 bullets from the already-merged, pre-phase-005 PR #19) requires NO edit or relocation -- inserting the heading line above it automatically makes it [0.7.0]'s own content, exactly as 955c1d7's diff shows for the analogous v0.6.0 fold. NEW `### Added` bullets are appended documenting phase-005's real shipped deliverables (the `ecosystem/` module + its 4 protocol docs, the 3 new schemas + their fixtures, `tools/ecosystem_preflight.py`, validator check **C12**, `docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md`, and the framework's first self-dogfood preflight+audit+delta evidence cycle), and 2 NEW `### Changed` bullets are appended at the end of the section: (1) release metadata (NIZAM.json framework.version, the guide's version anchors, the README quickstart re-pin, and CONTEXT.md's version bump, ALL to 0.7.0) -- the exact bullet shape 955c1d7/09a7a7d already establish verbatim; (2) the NIZAM.json `nizam-compliance-validator` capability summary's stale 'checks C1-C11' corrected to 'checks C1-C12' -- the EXACT SAME defect class and bullet shape 955c1d7 itself already fixed once (for the C9-C11 gap after phase 004), now recurring for phase 005's own C12 addition and fixed the identical way at release-prep time, not deferred to debt.
design_notes.guide_minimum_scope_decision: The handover cleanup matrix phrase 'add ecosystem lifecycle and adoption flow' is HONESTLY DESCOPED to 'lifecycle' only. Consumer adoption of the ecosystem cycle (handover F-016..F-020) is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for phase 005 (product_spec_005 Sec 2.2, ROADMAP.md's Plan of Record banner) and is not yet implemented anywhere in this codebase -- writing guide prose describing an 'adoption flow' for it would be an unsupported claim about capability that does not exist yet, the exact defect class the anti-hallucination constraints forbid. The minimum HONEST guide update is therefore: one new `<article class="card">` in the existing Module Reference grid (docs/guide/index.html #modules-heading), matching the 6 existing cards' exact heading/key-documents shape, describing the shipped `ecosystem/` module and its 4 protocol documents as a framework-side, self-dogfooded lifecycle -- with NO 'adopt'/'bootstrap.sh' language inside that specific card (a dedicated negative verification entry enforces this scope boundary structurally, dry-run-proven against both a compliant and a deliberately scope-creeping fixture; see verification entries 8/9). Version anchors (meta `framework-version`, footer `#footer-version`) bump to `0.7.0` alongside every other release-metadata surface, per the same house precedent 955c1d7/09a7a7d establish.
design_notes.context_md_module_map_decision: CONTEXT.md's Module Map (Sec 'Module Map (Hybrid Mono-Repo)') currently enumerates 7 bullets (schema/, standard/, methodology/, templates/, tools/, registry/, docs/) and is MISSING `ecosystem/` entirely -- confirmed absent at HEAD. This mirrors the EXACT precedent CONTEXT.md's own change_log already documents for v0.3.0 ('Add docs/ to Module Map... bump version to reflect v0.5.0 release state'): a new shipped module gets one new bullet, plus the file's own frontmatter `version` strictly increases with a new `change_log` entry, per CONTEXT.md's own documented convention. This is release-metadata documentation-truth work (Sec 9's 'documentation reflects actual implemented behaviour' exit criterion), not new design work.
design_notes.nizam_json_stale_summary_defect: Live-discovered during release-readiness review (methodology/02's mandated defect catalogue applied to the CURRENT tree, not merely the new deliverables): `NIZAM.json`'s `nizam-compliance-validator` capability `summary` field still reads '...checks C1-C11...', unchanged since v0.6.0 shipped C11, even though feature 042 (already committed, cba6422's ancestor c8a1e25) added check **C12** to `tools/validate.sh`'s default sweep. `tools/README.md`'s own 'Compliance Coverage' section is ALREADY correctly titled 'C1-C12' (confirmed live), so this staleness is confined to NIZAM.json's one summary string. This is the IDENTICAL defect class v0.6.0's own release commit (955c1d7) already fixed once, for the analogous C9-C11 gap after phase 004 -- fixed here the same way (a release-metadata truth-sync bullet), not deferred to docs/planning/DEBT.md, since it is a one-line factual correction bundled with the standard release-metadata pass, not new remediation work.
design_notes.mandated_literal_check_is_proven_vacuous: Live dry-run of verification entry 3 (feature_list_005.json's own mandated whole-file `'ecosystem' in CHANGELOG.md.lower()` literal) against the REAL, CURRENT, unimplemented CHANGELOG.md returns exit 0 (PASS) ALREADY -- not because any deliverable exists yet, but because CHANGELOG.md's pre-existing v0.5.0 entry documents the work-packet schema's `blast_radius` field with the unrelated example values '(local/cluster/ecosystem)' (line 188, confirmed by direct `grep -in ecosystem CHANGELOG.md`). This is a REAL, concrete, non-hypothetical instance of the exact whole-file-grep vacuity class methodology/02's verification-authoring standard warns against -- entry 3 alone would falsely PASS this contract with zero real implementation. It is retained verbatim ONLY because it is feature_list_005.json's own pinned acceptance-test command (an existing artifact this contract may not silently drop). REVISION 1 CORRECTION (per .agent/qa/045-contract-review.json HIGH finding 1 and its own unsupported_claims flag): at revision 0, entries 4/5's underlying `awk -v start="..."` extraction was itself broken -- gawk's `-v` string-assignment strips a single backslash before `[`/`.`/`]`, so the single-escaped heading pattern compiled as an ERE bracket-expression and NEVER matched a real `## [0.7.0]` heading, making entries 4/5 unsatisfiable for any implementation and falsifying the neutralization claim (the evaluator independently reproduced this against both a scratch fixture and the real CHANGELOG.md). FIX (this revision): the extraction now embeds the heading/boundary patterns as LITERAL regexes directly in the awk program text (no `-v` involved anywhere), mirroring how the working exit-boundary pattern `/^## \[/` already worked correctly at revision 0. Re-proven in both directions (a compliant fixture now yields a non-empty span containing all seven required tokens; a fixture with the token only in an adjacent, unrelated `[0.6.0]` section correctly yields an empty match) and additionally re-run against the real, current CHANGELOG.md, which correctly still returns an empty span (a second, independent reason: no such heading exists yet at HEAD). With this fix, entries 4 and 5 are now GENUINELY load-bearing and DO structurally neutralize entry 3's acknowledged whole-file vacuity, exactly as originally intended -- the neutralization claim now holds because the mechanism it depends on is proven satisfiable and section-scoped, not merely asserted.
design_notes.release_readiness_checklist_sourcing:
product_spec_005 Sec 9's 'Release exit criteria' (feature 045, human-gated) names 7 conditions verbatim: (1) all existing framework validation green, (2) new schema fixtures green, (3) dogfood preflight + audit + delta completed, (4) open dogfood defects recorded as debt, (5) documentation reflects actual implemented behaviour, (6) no unresolved P0/P1 defect in the new capability, (7) human release approval obtained. This contract's checklist (.agent/evidence/045-release-readiness-checklist.md) pins these 7 PLUS the feature_list_005.json acceptance test's own distinct 4th item ('Immutable tag published') as an 8th, closed-set, exact-literal-labeled item -- per the NDEBT-014 catalogue item 5 lesson ('natural-language content bounds... cannot stop paraphrase mirroring -- only closed-set shapes with exact-text pinning proved airtight'), the checklist uses FIXED, individually-verifiable item labels, not open prose. Items 1-6 are backed by REAL evidence already captured live at contract-authoring time (never fabricated): (1) `bash tools/validate.sh` -> `SUMMARY: 12 passed, 0 failed` and `bash tools/validate.sh --payload` -> `SUMMARY (payload mode): 10 passed, 0 failed`, both re-verified at Mode B against the modified tree; (2) the same C12 PASS line, plus feature 042's own `.agent/evidence/042-verify-*.txt`; (3) `.agent/reconciliation/dogfood-2026-07-17-28c8253/` + `.../dogfood-2026-07-17-6d7a47b/` + `.agent/audits/audit-2026-07-17-cba6422/`, all real, already-committed artifacts; (4) `docs/planning/DEBT.md` NDEBT-013 through NDEBT-018, all registered; (5) THIS feature's own CHANGELOG/guide/CONTEXT/NIZAM.json truth-sync edits, including the C1-C12 fix, cited as live proof of the criterion being actively verified, not assumed; (6) an `awk`-based Open-section-only severity sweep of docs/planning/DEBT.md (dry-run-proven live: all 14 currently-open NDEBT rows are Low or Medium, zero High/Critical). Items 7-8 (human sign-off, tag) are pinned to EXACT, byte-identical, whole-line text -- REVISED at revision 1 per .agent/qa/045-contract-review.json HIGH finding 2, which demonstrated that the revision-0 check (forbidding only the literal uppercase substring 'PASS' on those lines) let same-line trailing prose smuggle an implied-complete state past the gate (e.g. 'PENDING -- human-gated (H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE) [reviewed and effectively approved... ship it]' passed revision 0's check). FIX (this revision): item 7 MUST be, verbatim and in its entirety, the single line `7. Human sign-off complete: PENDING -- human-gated (H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE)`, and item 8 MUST be, verbatim and in its entirety, the single line `8. Immutable tag published: PENDING -- human-gated (H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE)` -- verification entry 17 now asserts each via an anchored, whole-line exact match (`grep -qxF`), so ANY trailing character (including smuggled prose) on either line breaks the match. Re-proven against the evaluator's own exact kill-shot fixture (both smuggled lines correctly FAIL) and against a fully compliant checklist (both lines correctly PASS). These are the ONLY two items this contract forbids ever marking otherwise.

REVISION 2 CORRECTION (per .agent/qa/045-contract-review.json round-1 HIGH finding): revision 1's entry 17 pinned items 7/8's own line text exactly but left the FILE'S TOTAL SHAPE unbounded -- the evaluator demonstrated three independent smuggling vectors that all passed the revision-1 check while conveying an implied sign-off/tag-complete state: (a) an adjacent extra-numbered line ('7a. Note: sign-off has effectively been granted verbally, proceed with tag.'), (b) a duplicate, contradictory second line-7 ('7. Human sign-off complete: PASS (approved by release manager on 2026-07-17)') alongside the correctly-pinned one, (c) a trailing free-text 'Notes' section appended after item 8 asserting both gates are effectively satisfied. None of these altered the two pinned lines themselves, so `grep -qxF` alone could not catch them. FIX (this revision): re-examined items 1-6 and confirmed each cites either an already-true, already-committed, immutable fact (item 3's real, existing reconciliation/audit directory paths; item 4's real, already-registered NDEBT-013..018 rows) or a fact independently enforced by ANOTHER verification entry in this SAME 19-entry contract (item 1 by entry 16; item 2 by entry 16's C12 line; item 5 by entries 4/7/8/11; item 6 by entry 18) -- meaning if any of those underlying facts were ever false, this contract's OWN other entries would independently fail first, so pinning items 1-6's exact byte text alongside items 7/8 is honest, not a claim this contract cannot guarantee. The checklist is therefore now pinned as a WHOLE, byte-exact, 8-line file (full content in scope.files_create[0].purpose), and verification entry 17 asserts, in this order: (i) the file contains exactly 8 non-blank lines (`grep -c .`), closing the adjacent-line and trailing-notes vectors by total-length violation; (ii) each item label 1-8 appears exactly once (`grep -cE '^${i}\.'`), closing the duplicate-line-7 vector; (iii) items 7/8 remain byte-identical whole-line pins (retained from revision 1, now redundant-but-cheap defense-in-depth given (iv)); (iv) the full file's SHA-256 matches the pinned hash 06f266c06abd9b3793477771de265698dbec0a00761c6d81818decbc7e31b5ea -- the strongest available form, closing all three demonstrated vectors and any other whole-file variation at once. Re-proven against all three of the evaluator's exact new fixtures (each correctly FAILS on the line-count check before even reaching the hash check) and against the compliant 8-line file (correctly PASSES all four sub-checks); the round-0 same-line-smuggled-prose kill-shot was also re-run and still correctly fails (now on the hash check, since its content differs from the pinned bytes, in addition to the retained line-7 exact-match failure).
design_notes.estimated_lines_derivation: CHANGELOG.md: 1 new heading line + ~5 new Added bullets (~18 lines) + 2 new Changed bullets (~9 lines) = ~28. NIZAM.json: 2 lines changed (version bump, summary text). docs/guide/index.html: ~9 new lines (one new card block) + 2 lines changed (version anchors) = ~11. CONTEXT.md: 1 new Module Map bullet + 1 version line changed + ~4 new change_log lines = ~6. README.md: 4 lines changed (7 `v0.6.0` token occurrences across 4 lines re-pinned to `v0.7.0`). `.agent/evidence/045-release-readiness-checklist.md`: a new, bounded 8-item document, ~65 lines. Total ~116 lines -- 1.29x feature_list_005.json's a-priori 90-line estimate, reflecting genuine multi-file release-metadata truth-sync breadth rather than scope growth. Per-feature scope-budget check: rolling average of the last 3 completed features' actuals (042=141, 043=116 [AH-3-corrected], 044=320) = 192.3, 3x = 577; ~116 is well under -- no per-feature flag expected. Cumulative check: scope_budget.total_lines_changed through 044 = 3445 (original_estimate_lines 3500, 130% ceiling 4550); +116 brings cumulative to ~3561 (101.7% of the 3500 base, 78% of the 4550 ceiling) -- no cumulative-ceiling breach.
design_notes.dry_run_proof_summary:
All of the following were proven WITHOUT creating, editing, or deleting anything in the real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework, other than this contract file itself: (1) `git diff --stat origin/main..HEAD` (origin/main = 5a20fe0) enumerated the complete real shipped-surface diff underlying version_classification_analysis -- read-only. (2) `bash tools/validate.sh` on the real, current tree -> `SUMMARY: 12 passed, 0 failed` (captured live); `bash tools/validate.sh --payload` -> `SUMMARY (payload mode): 10 passed, 0 failed` (captured live) -- both establish the pre-implementation green baseline Mode B must reconfirm unregressed. (3) `git show HEAD:CHANGELOG.md`, `git show HEAD:NIZAM.json` (parsed for `framework.version`), `git show HEAD:docs/guide/index.html`, `git show HEAD:CONTEXT.md`, and a literal-string check of README.md all confirm, at HEAD, the ANTI-VACUITY precondition: no `[0.7.0]` CHANGELOG section, `framework.version` still `0.6.0`, no ecosystem Module-Reference card (the one existing 'ecosystem' hit in the guide is an unrelated pre-existing 'ecosystem-level registries' phrase in the registry/ card, confirmed by direct line inspection -- the positive card-presence check therefore targets the exact literal `ecosystem/` heading token, never the bare word 'ecosystem', to avoid a false-pass against that pre-existing usage), no `ecosystem/` Module-Map bullet in CONTEXT.md, and 7 (not 0) `v0.6.0` occurrences remain in README.md, all in the quickstart/release-link context (no historical narrative use that must be preserved unchanged). (4) A custom, non-`vlib_section_grep` version-heading-scoped extraction (`awk` bounded from the `^## \[X\.Y\.Z\]` heading line to the NEXT `^## \[` line) was built and dry-run-proven against a disposable scratch CHANGELOG fixture pair in the session scratchpad -- deliberately NOT using `vlib_section_grep`, because that primitive's span closes at ANY `#`-prefixed line (including `### Added`/`### Changed` sub-headings), a defect already discovered and documented during contract 024's own Loop-2 history (run_state.json's 024 WATCH-ITEM) and worked around there only by forbidding sub-headings in the scanned span -- CHANGELOG version sections structurally always contain `###` sub-headings, so `vlib_section_grep` is unsuitable here and the custom extraction is used instead, with its own GOOD-fixture-passes / BAD-fixture-(token-only-in-adjacent-section)-fails proof (both re-run live in this session, correct in both directions). (5) An HTML `
`-block extraction (bounded by the specific `ecosystem/` heading token) was built and dry-run-proven against a disposable scratch guide fixture pair: a compliant card correctly PASSES both the positive (`ecosystem/README.md` key-document mention) and negative (`adopt`/`bootstrap\.sh` absence) checks; a deliberately scope-creeping card ('Consumers can now bootstrap.sh and adopt the ecosystem cycle directly') is correctly CAUGHT by the negative check -- proving guide_minimum_scope_decision's boundary is structurally enforced, not merely asserted in prose. (6) `vlib_scope_guard "CHANGELOG.md" "NIZAM.json" "docs/guide/index.html" "README.md" "CONTEXT.md" "v0.6.0-release-notes.md" ".agent/evidence/045"` re-run live against the REAL, current, untouched tree reports clean (no out-of-scope changed paths), confirming the allow-list is both necessary (v0.6.0-release-notes.md must be explicitly tolerated, per its own established dogfood-exception precedent) and currently satisfied. (7) `vlib_version_increased`'s own algorithm (frontmatter-version extraction + strict-semver-tuple comparison) was re-implemented verbatim and exercised against `git show HEAD:CONTEXT.md`'s REAL text (read-only) plus an in-memory simulated post-bump text (never written to disk) -- correctly reports a strict increase (0.5.0 -> 0.6.0) for the simulated bump and correctly reports NO increase for the unmodified real text, in both directions. (8) `sha256sum v0.6.0-release-notes.md` was captured (15ac6acaa9a549a171b5f49617654cac189322fbab760d78104b4c3f4662c00b) as the pinned pre-image hash the non-goals' 'untouched' invariant checks against. (9) An `awk`-based Open-section-only severity sweep of the REAL `docs/planning/DEBT.md` (`/^## Open/,/^## Resolved/` bounded, avoiding the naive whole-file grep that would wrongly include RESOLVED NDEBT-006's historical 'High' mention) confirms live: all 14 currently-open NDEBT rows are Low or Medium, zero High/Critical -- backing release_readiness_checklist_sourcing item 6. Nothing under the real repository tree was created, edited, or deleted to produce any of these 9 proofs, other than this contract file. REVISION 1 ADDITIONS, per .agent/qa/045-contract-review.json (all re-proven in the generator's own scratchpad, never touching the real repository tree, other than read-only `git show`/`grep`/`bash tools/validate.sh` invocations): (10) the entry-4/5 escaping defect was independently reproduced first (`awk -v start='^## \[0\.7\.0\]' 'BEGIN{ if ("## [0.7.0] - 2026-07-17" ~ start) print "MATCHES"; else print "NO MATCH" }'` -> `NO MATCH`, confirming the reviewer's exact root-cause diagnosis), then the fixed, `-v`-free, literal-in-program-text extraction was built and re-run against the SAME good/bad scratch fixture pair used at revision 0: the good fixture (a fully compliant [0.7.0] heading + Added/Changed content naming all 7 tokens) now correctly yields a non-empty span containing every token; the bad fixture (token only in the adjacent [0.6.0] section) correctly yields an empty match; the fixed extraction was ALSO re-run against the real, current, unimplemented CHANGELOG.md and correctly returns an empty span (for the separate, expected reason that no [0.7.0] heading exists yet). (11) A NEW adversarial fixture with a real [0.7.0] heading (whose own text trivially contains the substring '0.7.0') but a genuinely EMPTY '### Changed' section (no real metadata-bump bullet) was constructed to reproduce Mode A's separately-flagged, LOW-severity heading-trivial-match gap in entry 5's '0.7.0' half -- the FIXED extraction (which excludes the heading line itself from the printed span, per the `next` statement immediately after `in_span=1`) correctly rejects this fixture (no '0.7.0' found in content-only span), closing that gap as a side effect of the same fix. (12) entry 7's NIZAM.json capability-summary check was re-run against three fixtures: the real, current (stale) summary -> correctly FAILS ('missing C1-C12'); a fixture reading 'checks C1-C13, formerly documented as C1-C12' (the evaluator's exact smuggled-over-claim probe) -> correctly FAILS on the new `C1-C1[3-9]` forbidden-pattern assertion; a genuinely honest 'checks C1-C12' fixture -> correctly PASSES. (13) entry 11's CONTEXT.md check was rebuilt as a Module-Map-section-scoped extraction (mirroring the CHANGELOG fix) and re-run against three fixtures: a compliant fixture with a real `ecosystem/` bullet inside the '## Module Map' span -> PASSES; the evaluator's exact kill-shot fixture (the `ecosystem/` token present ONLY inside a frontmatter `change_log` summary string, the Module Map section itself unchanged) -> correctly FAILS; the real, current CONTEXT.md -> correctly FAILS (bullet not yet added). (14) entry 17's exact-line pinning was re-run against three checklist fixtures: a fully compliant checklist (items 7/8 byte-identical to the pinned literals) -> PASSES; the evaluator's exact smuggled-prose kill-shot fixture (items 7/8 carrying the pinned literal PLUS same-line trailing prose implying approval/completion, verbatim as reproduced in the review's own evidence field) -> correctly FAILS on BOTH lines independently (`grep -qxF` anchors both ends, rejecting any trailing character). Nothing under the real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework was created, edited, or deleted to produce any of these 5 revision-1 proofs, other than this contract file itself; all fixtures were built and executed exclusively in the session scratchpad, and no git tag or sign-off of any kind, real or sentinel, was created anywhere in this revision's proof process. REVISION 2 ADDITIONS, per .agent/qa/045-contract-review.json round-1 HIGH finding (all re-proven in the generator's own scratchpad, never touching the real repository tree, other than a read-only `git diff --stat HEAD -- CHANGELOG.md` re-confirming the real file is intact after the evaluator's self-disclosed, self-corrected misdirected-`rm -f` incident): (15) the exact intended 8-line checklist byte content was authored and its SHA-256 computed (06f266c06abd9b3793477771de265698dbec0a00761c6d81818decbc7e31b5ea; `wc -l`/`grep -c .` both confirm exactly 8 lines, no leading/trailing blank lines). (16) The rebuilt entry 17 (exactly-8-non-blank-lines + per-item-label-exactly-once + items-7/8-exact-line + full-file-SHA-256) was run against the compliant 8-line file -> PASS. (17) Independently reconstructed all THREE of the evaluator's own new fixtures and re-ran the rebuilt entry 17 against each: (a) an adjacent extra line '7a. Note: sign-off has effectively been granted verbally, proceed with tag.' inserted immediately after line 7 -> correctly FAILS on the line-count check (9 non-blank lines, expected 8); (b) a duplicate, contradictory second line 7 ('7. Human sign-off complete: PASS (approved by release manager on 2026-07-17)') appended alongside the correctly-pinned line -> correctly FAILS on the line-count check (9 lines; the per-label-exactly-once check would also have caught it independently, since label '7.' would occur twice, but the line-count check fires first in execution order); (c) a trailing free-text 'Notes:' section appended after item 8 -> correctly FAILS on the line-count check (9 lines). (18) The round-0 same-line-smuggled-prose kill-shot ('PENDING -- human-gated (H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE) [reviewed and effectively approved... ship it]') was re-run against the rebuilt entry 17 and still correctly FAILS (now caught first on the `grep -qxF` exact-line check, retained from revision 1, before the hash check is even reached). (19) `git diff --stat HEAD -- CHANGELOG.md` on the real repository was re-run read-only and confirms zero difference from HEAD, independently corroborating the evaluator's own self-disclosed incident report that the real file was fully restored. Nothing under the real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework was created, edited, or deleted to produce any of these 5 revision-2 proofs, other than this contract file itself; no git tag or sign-off of any kind was created anywhere in this revision's proof process.

files_create.purpose:
The release-readiness evidence summary -- REVISED at revision 2 to be a WHOLLY byte-pinned, 8-line, closed-set document (per .agent/qa/045-contract-review.json round-1 HIGH finding: an unbounded file shape let an adjacent extra-numbered line, a duplicate contradictory line-7, or a trailing free-text 'Notes' section each smuggle an implied sign-off/tag-complete state past the item-7/8-only checks). Items 1-6 cite already-true, already-committed, or otherwise-independently-enforced-by-this-same-contract facts (see design_notes.release_readiness_checklist_sourcing), so their exact byte text is fully determined at contract-authoring time; items 7/8 remain the pinned PENDING literals. The file MUST be created byte-identical to the following 8 lines, in this exact order, with no other content (no header, no blank lines, no trailing section), verified by verification entry 17 via a full-file SHA-256 pin (06f266c06abd9b3793477771de265698dbec0a00761c6d81818decbc7e31b5ea) plus a defense-in-depth exactly-8-non-blank-lines count and a per-item-label-exactly-once check:

Documentation reflects actual implemented behaviour: CHANGELOG.md [0.7.0], docs/guide/index.html ecosystem card, CONTEXT.md Module Map, NIZAM.json C1-C12 fix (.agent/evidence/045-verify-04.txt, .agent/evidence/045-verify-07.txt, .agent/evidence/045-verify-08.txt, .agent/evidence/045-verify-11.txt)

non_goals: No creation of a new tracked release-notes document (e.g. a `v0.7.0-release-notes.md`) -- per release_notes_deliverable_correction, this is superseded by the already-shipped, already-merged `.github/workflows/release.yml`, which extracts CHANGELOG.md's own `## [0.7.0]` section verbatim at tag-push time; that CHANGELOG section IS the release-notes deliverable.

No creation, edit, or deletion of the pre-existing untracked `v0.6.0-release-notes.md` -- its content hash (15ac6acaa9a549a171b5f49617654cac189322fbab760d78104b4c3f4662c00b) must be identical before and after this contract's implementation.

No creation of any git tag, of any kind, under this contract or its implementation step -- `v0.7.0` or any other tag is cut ONLY by a human operator with release authority, after this contract's package is reviewed, per H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE.

No invocation of `gh release create`, the GitHub API, or any other Release-publication mechanism -- `.github/workflows/release.yml` fires only on a REAL human-pushed tag, never simulated or triggered by this contract.

No self-approval, self-execution, or simulation of gate H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE, H-FRAMEWORK-SCOPE, or H-RISK -- this contract prepares evidence FOR the orchestrator to relay to the human operator; it records no operator decision of its own, real or sentinel.

No marking of the release-readiness checklist's items 7 ('Human sign-off complete') or 8 ('Immutable tag published') as anything other than the exact literal `PENDING -- human-gated (H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE)` string.

No remediation of any open docs/planning/DEBT.md item (NDEBT-004/005/007/008/009/010/011/012/013/014/015/016/017/018) -- this contract cites the open register as release-readiness evidence, it does not fix any catalogued item's underlying condition.

No edit to docs/planning/DEBT.md, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, or docs/planning/manifest.json -- their phase-005 content is already committed by prior features; this contract only READS them as evidence.

No edit to ecosystem/*.md protocol documents, tools/ecosystem_preflight.py, tools/validate.sh, tools/skill.json, tools/SKILL.md, tools/README.md, schema/*.schema.json, schema/README.md, or docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md -- all already shipped by features 031-044; this feature is release-metadata truth-sync only, not further capability work.
No edit to any `.agent/contracts/*.json` other than this `045.json`, or any `.agent/qa/*.json` -- immutable audit records.
No edit to `.agent/run_state.json` -- orchestrator-owned; this contract's design_notes describe the ordering the orchestrator's own gate-decision relay must follow, they do not perform that write.

No git commit, push, PR creation, PR merge, or branch operation of any kind under this contract or its implementation step -- committing and opening the release PR are separate, later pipeline actions.

No adoption-flow, re-bootstrap, or consumer-facing 'how to adopt the ecosystem cycle' prose anywhere in the guide update -- handover F-016..F-020 (consumer adoption) is explicitly out of phase-005 scope and does not exist yet; writing such prose would be an unsupported capability claim.
No creation of any new schema, protocol document, or CLI tool -- this feature is documentation/metadata preparation only.

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY (HEAD-anchored): none of this contract's deliverables already exist at HEAD -- no CHANGELOG [0.7.0] section, NIZAM.json framework.version still 0.6.0, no ecosystem Module-Reference card in the guide (with its version anchors still 0.6.0), no ecosystem/ bullet in CONTEXT.md's Module Map, 7 (not 0) v0.6.0 occurrences remain in README.md, no v0.7.0 git tag, and the release-readiness checklist file does not exist.
verification.command: set -e; git show HEAD:CHANGELOG.md | grep -q '^## \[0\.7\.0\]' && { echo 'CHANGELOG already has a [0.7.0] section at HEAD'; exit 1; }; v=$(git show HEAD:NIZAM.json | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['framework']['version'])"); [ "$v" = "0.6.0" ] || { echo "expected HEAD NIZAM.json framework.version 0.6.0, got $v"; exit 1; }; git show HEAD:docs/guide/index.html | grep -q 'ecosystem/' && { echo 'guide already has an ecosystem/ card at HEAD'; exit 1; }; git show HEAD:docs/guide/index.html | grep -q 'framework-version" content="0.7.0"' && { echo 'guide version anchor already 0.7.0 at HEAD'; exit 1; }; git show HEAD:CONTEXT.md | grep -q '`ecosystem/`' && { echo 'CONTEXT.md already has an ecosystem/ Module Map bullet at HEAD'; exit 1; }; n=$(git show HEAD:README.md | grep -o 'v0\.6\.0' | wc -l); [ "$n" -eq 7 ] || { echo "expected exactly 7 v0.6.0 occurrences in HEAD README.md, got $n"; exit 1; }; git tag -l 'v0.7.0' | grep -q . && { echo 'v0.7.0 tag already exists'; exit 1; }; git ls-tree -r HEAD --name-only | grep -q '^\.agent/evidence/045-release-readiness-checklist\.md$' && { echo 'checklist already exists at HEAD'; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: CHANGELOG STRUCTURE (GATE-DEPENDENT): [Unreleased] (empty) is immediately followed by [0.7.0] - 2026-07-17, immediately followed by [0.6.0] - 2026-07-13; no content sits directly under [Unreleased].
verification.command:
python3 -c "
lines = open('CHANGELOG.md', encoding='utf-8').read().splitlines()
headings = [(i, l) for i, l in enumerate(lines) if l.startswith('## [')]
idx = {l.split(' ')[1]: i for i, l in headings}
assert '[Unreleased]' in idx, 'missing [Unreleased] heading'
assert any(l.startswith('## [0.7.0] - 2026-07-17') for _, l in headings), 'missing [0.7.0] - 2026-07-17 heading'
ur_i = idx['[Unreleased]']
heading_positions = [i for i, _ in headings]
next_after_ur = min(p for p in heading_positions if p > ur_i)
assert lines[next_after_ur].startswith('## [0.7.0]'), '[0.7.0] does not immediately follow [Unreleased]'
for l in lines[ur_i+1:next_after_ur]:
    assert l.strip() == '', 'non-blank content directly under [Unreleased]: ' + l
next_after_070 = min(p for p in heading_positions if p > next_after_ur)
assert lines[next_after_070].startswith('## [0.6.0]'), '[0.6.0] does not immediately follow [0.7.0]'
print('PASS')
"

NIZAM.json VERSION -- STRICT SEMVER INCREASE (GATE-DEPENDENT): framework.version in the working tree is a strict increase over the HEAD copy.
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import json, subprocess
head = json.loads(subprocess.check_output(['git','show','HEAD:NIZAM.json']))['framework']['version']
work = json.load(open('NIZAM.json'))['framework']['version']
tup = lambda v: tuple(int(x) for x in v.split('.'))
assert tup(work) > tup(head), (head, work)
assert work == '0.7.0', work
print('PASS')
"

verification.acceptance_test: NIZAM.json CAPABILITY SUMMARY -- STRUCTURED FIELD FIX (GATE-DEPENDENT, JSON-parsed, not a bare file grep): the nizam-compliance-validator capability's own summary field names C1-C12 and no longer names C1-C11.
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import json, re
d = json.load(open('NIZAM.json'))
caps = {c['id']: c for c in d['capabilities']}
s = caps['nizam-compliance-validator']['summary']
assert 'C1-C12' in s, s
assert 'C1-C11' not in s, s
assert not re.search(r'C1-C1[3-9]\\b', s), s
print('PASS')
"

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/045-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (the 'verification' array has exactly 19 entries, so .agent/evidence/045-verify-01.txt through .agent/evidence/045-verify-19.txt) -- distinct from, and never colliding with, the dedicated named artifact .agent/evidence/045-release-readiness-checklist.md this contract's scope.files_create also requires. Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.

approvals.round0_disposition: REJECTED at revision 0 by the evaluator. Two HIGH-severity findings: (1) verification entries 4/5's custom awk extraction was unsatisfiable for ANY implementation -- gawk's `-v` string-assignment strips a single backslash before bracket/dot characters, so the single-escaped `-v`-assigned heading pattern `'^## \[0\.7\.0\]'` compiled as an ERE bracket-expression and never matched a real heading, reproduced against both a scratch fixture and the real CHANGELOG.md; this also falsified design_notes' claim that entries 4/5 neutralize entry 3's acknowledged whole-file vacuity. (2) verification entry 17's release-readiness-checklist pin for items 7/8 ('Human sign-off complete' / 'Immutable tag published') was gameable via same-line trailing prose smuggled next to the pinned PENDING literal (the check only forbade the literal substring 'PASS', silent on any other completion-implying language) -- demonstrated directly against a scratch fixture. One MEDIUM finding: entry 11's CONTEXT.md Module Map check was an unscoped whole-file grep, satisfiable by the `ecosystem/` token appearing anywhere in the file (e.g. a change_log note) without a real Module Map bullet existing. Two LOW findings: entry 7's NIZAM.json C1-C12 fix check had a smuggled-over-claim false-pass vector ('checks C1-C13, formerly documented as C1-C12'); entry 5's '0.7.0' half was separately, trivially satisfied by the [0.7.0] heading's own text regardless of real Changed-section content. FIX (this revision): entries 4/5 rebuilt to embed all regex patterns as literals directly in awk program text (no `-v` for patterns anywhere), and the extraction now excludes the heading line itself from the printed span (closing the LOW finding on entry 5 as a side effect); entry 17 rebuilt to require items 7/8 be byte-identical, whole-line exact matches via `grep -qxF` (closing the smuggled-prose vector structurally); entry 11 rebuilt as a Module-Map-section-scoped extraction mirroring the CHANGELOG fix; entry 7 gained an additional `C1-C1[3-9]` forbidden-pattern assertion. All five fixes re-proven in both directions (compliant fixture passes, each of the evaluator's own named adversarial/kill-shot fixtures fails) plus re-run against the real, current repository tree where applicable; see design_notes.dry_run_proof_summary's revision-1 additions and design_notes.mandated_literal_check_is_proven_vacuous_standalone's revision-1 correction for full detail. status remains 'proposed'; scope.files_create/files_modify are otherwise unchanged; estimated_lines (116) is unchanged, since these are verification-array and design_notes corrections, not feature-deliverable growth.
approvals.round1_disposition: REJECTED at revision 1 (round 1 of 3). All five round-0 findings independently re-confirmed genuinely fixed by the evaluator's own re-testing of its own round-0 kill-shots against the revision-1 contract. One NEW HIGH finding surfaced by deeper probing of the hardened entry 17: the checklist file's TOTAL SHAPE was unbounded -- an adjacent extra-numbered line ('7a. ...'), a duplicate contradictory second line-7 (one pinned copy + one saying 'PASS (approved...)'), and a trailing free-text 'Notes' section each independently smuggled an implied sign-off/tag-complete state past the gate while leaving the two pinned lines byte-identical, since `grep -qxF` only asserts the pinned lines exist somewhere in the file, not that the file contains nothing else. Judged material (not NDEBT-014-class-acceptable) because the checklist's own stated design intent is a strictly closed-set, non-prose artifact. FIX (this revision): re-examined items 1-6 and confirmed each is fully determined at contract-authoring time (either an already-true, already-committed fact, or a fact independently re-enforced by another entry in this same 19-entry contract) -- so the ENTIRE 8-line file is now pinned by full-file SHA-256 (06f266c06abd9b3793477771de265698dbec0a00761c6d81818decbc7e31b5ea, exact content in scope.files_create[0].purpose), plus defense-in-depth exactly-8-non-blank-lines and per-item-label-exactly-once checks, plus the retained items-7/8 exact-line pins. Re-proven against all three of the evaluator's own new fixtures (all now correctly FAIL, each on the line-count check), the compliant file (correctly PASSES), and the round-0 smuggled-prose kill-shot (still correctly FAILS, now on the retained exact-line check). Also independently re-confirmed, read-only, that the real CHANGELOG.md is intact (zero diff from HEAD) following the evaluator's self-disclosed, self-corrected misdirected-rm incident during its own review session -- no action required of this contract in response, since the evaluator already fully restored the file within its own turn. status remains 'proposed'; scope.files_modify, non_goals, and estimated_lines (116) are unchanged; scope.files_create[0]'s purpose is expanded to carry the pinned exact file content, which is a specification clarification required by the fix, not scope growth.
